items.title: U.S. House passes affordable housing bill, sending it to Trump for enactment
items.description: The high cost ​of living in the U.S., with the inflation rate rising significantly during President Trump’s second term in ⁠office, is ranked as a top worry by voters in ⁠public opinion polls.

items.title: More than a unibrow: The enduring appeal of Frida Kahlo
items.description: She shunned feminine beauty ideals, was bisexual and a political radical, all while being brutally honest in her art. A major Frida Kahlo exhibition opens at London's Tate Modern.

items.title: U.S. Senate passes bipartisan housing bill aimed at increasing supply, lowering prices
items.description: The bill, which passed 85-5 and now heads to the House, has been the focus of intense negotiations in recent weeks as lawmakers in both parties try to address housing costs in an election year

items.title: Sir Keir Starmer resigns as UK PM, Andy Burnham favourite to take over
items.description: Andy Burnham, who secured a return to Parliament just last week, appeared poised to succeed Starmer as Labour leader and UK PM after would-be rival Wes Streeting offered him his support. Leer más

items.title: Milei leads rightist leaders congratulating Colombian president-elect
items.description: Argentina's President Javier Milei one of the first to congratulate Colombia's hard-right president-elect Abelardo de la Espriella. Leer más

items.title: Anthropic says Alibaba illicitly extracted Claude AI model capabilities
Argentina’s economy grew more than expected in first quarter
items.description: Gross domestic product expanded 0.7% compared to the previous quarter, above analyst forecasts, even as unemployment rose and wages trailed inflation. Leer más

items.title: Is xenophobia in South Africa risking investment?
items.description: Vigilante groups and right-wing populist forces are stoking anti-immigrant sentiment and demanding that they leave the country by June 30. Experts fear serious economic implications for the country and the region.

items.title: How Africa's youth wants to save democracy
items.description: Africa's young population often finds itself facing aging, autocratic rulers. Unwilling to settle for democratic rituals alone, many are searching for creative new ways to shape their future.

items.title: LPPC releases short list of 89 candidates for 2026 SAN conferment (FULL LIST)
items.description: The LPPC cautioned that inclusion on the short list does not amount to a successful application for the SAN rank. The post LPPC releases short list of 89 candidates for 2026 SAN conferment (FULL LIST) appeared first on Premium Times Nigeria .

items.title: UN adopts resolution upholding accountability for attacks on peacekeepers
items.description: It authorises new steps to ensure that those who commit crimes against peacekeepers face justice.

items.title: Palestinian activist faints after his release from Israeli prison
items.description: Palestinian activist Abdullah Shatat collapsed moments after his release from an Israeli jail.

items.title: DR Congo: Mining operations continue at epicentre of Ebola outbreak
items.description: At a gold mine in Mongbwalu, necessity is forcing people to put aside their fear of the virus and carry on working.

items.title: U.S. lifts Iran oil sanctions. And, federal judge rules SAVE voter tool unlawful
items.description: The U.S. has temporarily lifted oil sanctions on Iran as peace talks continue. And, a federal judge ruled that the Trump administration's data system, known as SAVE, is unlawful.

items.title: LPPC suspends senior lawyer Mike Ozekhome from SAN rank
items.description: The LPPC said Mr Ozekhome's suspension from using the SAN rank is "pending the final determination of disciplinary proceedings currently before its Disciplinary and Ethics Sub-Committee and other related proceedings". The post LPPC suspends senior lawyer Mike Ozekhome from SAN rank appeared first on Premium Times Nigeria .

items.title: Trading Day: Tech jitters smother inflation relief
Trump offers Iran an economic lifeline — with strings
items.description: Iran has seen a speedy rebound in oil exports after the US-Iran deal granted immediate waivers on US sanctions. Relief on frozen assets and a $300 billion reconstruction fund now hinge on Tehran meeting key conditions.

items.title: Meet Andy Burnham, Britain's likely next prime minister
items.description: Now that British Prime Minister Keir Starmer announced his resignation, all eyes are on Andy Burnham. The former mayor of Manchester is seen as the favorite to succeed Starmer to lead the United Kingdom.

items.title: South Africa's appeals court grants Lungu's family burial right
items.description: A legal battle over where former Zambian President Edgar Lungu's remains will be buried may be over, more than a year after he died, as South Africa's Supreme Court of Appeal ruled Tuesday in favor of his family and rejected the Zambian government's claim of custody over his body.

items.title: France reports first Congo outbreak linked Ebola case
items.description: France has confirmed its first Ebola case linked to the outbreak in the Democratic Republic of Congo. The infection comes weeks after European health authorities said there were no active Ebola cases in the EU.

items.title: New dollar-cash videos put Insaurralde case back into spotlight
items.description: Videos allegedly showing hidden dollar stashes surface from Martín Insaurralde case; Footage leaked by 'La Nación' shows Jesica Cirio exposing secret stashes kept by veteran Peronist. Leer más

items.title: Former Liberal leader Matthew Guy denies assaulting MP Moira Deeming
items.description: Victorian MP Matthew Guy is considering legal advice to sue his party room colleague for defamation over the assault allegation, which is being investigated by police.

items.title: World Cup 2026: Vinicius Junior scores twice as Brazil beat Scotland to reach last 32
items.description: Vinicius Junior scored twice as Brazil eased to a 3-0 win over Scotland on Wednesday, sealing top spot in Group C and a place in the World Cup last 32. Matheus Cunha added a third, while Neymar made his first international appearance in more than two-and-a-half years after returning from injury.

items.title: Why Austrian author Ingeborg Bachmann remains a literary icon
items.description: A new documentary featuring Sandra Hüller celebrates the Austrian poet and author who was a literary star of her era. She would have turned 100 this year.

items.title: France heatwave's first major outage leaves 68,000 homes without power
items.description: “The outage was related to extreme temperatures and did not injure anyone,” the prefecture in the coastal department of Finistere said in a statement

items.title: Blood ties and opportunity: Why more World Cup players than ever are not representing their birth nations
items.description: Nearly a quarter of footballers at the 2026 tournament are not playing for their country of birth.

items.title: Fact check: AI fakes around the World Cup spread political narratives
items.description: Keir Starmer in a Croatia jersey. An Iranian protest against a US strike. Calls to arrest Brazil's President Lula. Adolf Hitler in the German fan section — all supposedly seen at the World Cup. But none of it is real.
